one_time_description: CMHC Rapid Housing Initiative funding received for development of affordable housing, recorded as deferred revenue
economic_notes: Band owns interests in LNIB Aggregates LP (aggregate activities, 50.99% interest in pipeline maintenance/construction partnerships), LNIB Holdings LP, NAIK Development LP, Shulus Forest Enterprises LP (forestry), LNIB Ventures LP (revenue sharing joint ventures), Shulus Electricity Transmission Line LP, Shulus Farms LP (hay cultivation). LNIB Aggregates LP had revenues of $58.9M in 2022. Band has guaranteed line of credit for Lower Nicola Site Services LP and Ogilvie Mountain Holdings LP up to $5,565,000.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to incomplete tangible capital asset records - certain historical assets missing from TCA schedule, effects undetermined. LNIB Development Corporation is fully consolidated subsidiary. Six partnerships accounted for on modified equity basis (GBEs). Large deferred revenue of $6,655,000 from CMHC Rapid Housing Initiative. Income from investment in band-owned entities of $11,759,593 is a significant non-cash revenue item driven by LNIB Aggregates LP pipeline work. Wages per segmented schedule ($9,908,058) differs slightly from expenditures by object ($9,623,227) - segmented figure includes LNIBDC wages. OSR calculated as total revenue minus ISC + CMHC + FNHA + FNESC + Province of BC + BC First Nations Gaming = $39,034,298 - $13,872,277 = $25,162,021. total_government_transfers = ISC $8,233,313 + CMHC $1,318,524 + FNHA $1,482,598 + FNESC $416,240 + Province of BC $1,023,612 + BC First Nations Gaming $338,390 = $13,872,677 (rounding used $13,872,277 based on line sum).
